A museum is a building displaying collections of artifacts, art, or objects of cultural or historical interest.
A zoo is a facility where animals are kept in enclosures for people to see and learn about wildlife.
A park is a public outdoor space with grass, trees, and facilities for recreation and relaxation.
A playground is an outdoor area with equipment like swings and slides for children to play on.
A stadium is a large venue for sports events or concerts with seating for many spectators.
A theater is a building where plays, movies, or performances are presented to an audience.
A restaurant is a business where people pay to sit and eat meals prepared by cooks.
A store (or shop) is a building where goods are sold to customers.
A mall is a large building with many stores, restaurants, and entertainment options under one roof.
A market is a place where people buy and sell goods, often food and produce.
A farm is land used for growing crops or raising animals for food and other products.
Agriculture is the science and practice of farming - growing crops and raising livestock.
A crop is a plant grown in large quantities for food or other uses, like corn or wheat.
Harvest is the process of gathering mature crops from fields at the end of the growing season.
Livestock are farm animals raised for food, fiber, or labor, like cows, pigs, and chickens.
A pet is a domestic animal kept for companionship and enjoyment, like dogs, cats, or birds.
A dog is a domesticated mammal often kept as a pet, known for loyalty and companionship with humans.
A cat is a small domesticated carnivorous mammal, popular as a pet for its independence and affection.
A bird is a warm-blooded vertebrate with feathers, wings, a beak, and typically the ability to fly.
A fish is a cold-blooded aquatic vertebrate with gills for breathing underwater and fins for swimming.
A reptile is a cold-blooded vertebrate with scaly skin, like snakes, lizards, turtles, and crocodiles.
An amphibian is a cold-blooded vertebrate that lives in water and on land, like frogs and salamanders.
An insect is a small invertebrate with six legs and three body parts, like ants, bees, and butterflies.
A butterfly is a flying insect with large colorful wings and a slender body, formed from a caterpillar.
A bee is a flying insect that collects nectar, pollinates flowers, and makes honey in colonies.
An ant is a small social insect living in organized colonies with distinct worker, queen, and soldier roles.
A spider is an eight-legged arachnid that spins webs to catch prey and is found almost everywhere.
A tree is a tall plant with a wooden trunk, branches, and leaves that can live for many years.
A flower is the reproductive part of a plant, often colorful and fragrant to attract pollinators.
Grass is a green plant with narrow leaves, covering the ground in lawns, fields, and meadows.
A seed is a small plant embryo in a protective coating, capable of growing into a new plant.
A root is the underground part of a plant that absorbs water and nutrients and anchors it in soil.
A leaf is a flat green organ of a plant where photosynthesis occurs, making food for the plant.
A stem is the main structural part of a plant that supports leaves, flowers, and transports nutrients.
Fruit is the seed-bearing structure of flowering plants, often sweet and edible, like apples or oranges.
A vegetable is an edible plant or part of a plant, like roots, stems, or leaves, used as food.
An apple is a round fruit with red, green, or yellow skin and white crisp flesh, growing on trees.
A banana is a long curved yellow fruit with soft sweet flesh, growing in clusters on tropical plants.
An orange is a round citrus fruit with orange skin and juicy sweet-tart flesh, rich in vitamin C.
A strawberry is a small red fruit with tiny seeds on its surface and sweet juicy flesh.
A carrot is an orange root vegetable, crunchy and sweet, high in vitamins and often eaten raw or cooked.
A potato is a starchy tuber vegetable growing underground, versatile and used in many dishes worldwide.
Rice is a cereal grain that is a staple food for much of the world's population, especially in Asia.
Bread is a baked food made from flour, water, and yeast, forming a staple in many cultures.
Cheese is a dairy product made from milk, available in many varieties with different flavors and textures.
Milk is a white liquid produced by mammals, rich in nutrients and calcium, often from cows.
Chocolate is a sweet food made from cacao seeds, popular as candy, dessert, and drink flavoring.
Ice cream is a frozen dessert made from dairy, sugar, and flavorings, enjoyed in many flavors worldwide.
A cake is a sweet baked dessert made from flour, sugar, eggs, and butter, often frosted and decorated.
A cookie is a small sweet baked treat, typically flat and round, made with flour, sugar, and butter.
Candy is a sweet confection made primarily from sugar, available in countless flavors and forms.
Breakfast is the first meal of the day, typically eaten in the morning after waking up.
Lunch is the midday meal, eaten around noon, usually lighter than dinner.
Dinner is the main meal of the day, typically eaten in the evening with family or friends.
A snack is a small amount of food eaten between main meals to satisfy hunger.
Thirst is the body's signal that you need to drink water or other fluids for hydration.
Hunger is the physical sensation indicating your body needs food for energy and nutrients.
Taste is one of the five senses, detecting flavors through taste buds on the tongue.
Smell is one of the five senses, detecting odors through receptors in the nose.
Touch is one of the five senses, feeling textures, temperatures, and pressure through the skin.
Sight is one of the five senses, perceiving visual information through the eyes.
Hearing is one of the five senses, perceiving sounds through the ears.
The brain is the organ controlling thoughts, memory, emotions, and all body functions from inside the skull.
The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood throughout the body, supplying oxygen and nutrients.
Blood is the red fluid circulating through the body, carrying oxygen, nutrients, and removing waste.
Lungs are two organs in the chest that allow us to breathe by taking in oxygen and releasing carbon dioxide.
Breathing is inhaling air into the lungs and exhaling it out, providing oxygen to the body.
Bones are hard tissues forming the skeleton, supporting the body and protecting internal organs.
Muscles are tissues that contract to produce movement, allowing us to walk, run, and perform actions.
Skin is the outer covering of the body, protecting internal organs and regulating temperature.
Eyes are organs of sight that detect light and send visual information to the brain.
Ears are organs that detect sound waves and help us hear and maintain balance.
The nose is the organ for smelling and the primary entrance for air into the respiratory system.
The mouth is used for eating, drinking, speaking, and is the beginning of the digestive system.
Teeth are hard structures in the mouth used to bite and chew food before swallowing.
The tongue is a muscular organ in the mouth used for tasting, swallowing, and speaking.
Hands are the end parts of arms with fingers, used for grasping, touching, and manipulating objects.
Fingers are the five digits on each hand that allow precise gripping and manipulation of objects.
Feet are the end parts of legs used for standing, walking, and supporting the body's weight.
Toes are the five digits on each foot that help with balance and pushing off when walking.
Legs are the lower limbs used for standing, walking, running, and supporting body weight.
Arms are the upper limbs extending from shoulders to hands, used for reaching and carrying.
The stomach is a digestive organ that breaks down food using acids and enzymes before it enters the intestines.
Digestion is the process of breaking down food into nutrients that the body can absorb and use.
Health is the state of being free from illness or injury, with physical and mental wellbeing.
Medicine is the science and practice of diagnosing, treating, and preventing disease and injury.
A doctor is a medical professional trained to diagnose and treat illnesses and injuries in patients.
A nurse is a healthcare professional who cares for patients and assists doctors in medical treatment.
A disease is a condition that impairs normal body functioning, often causing specific symptoms.
A virus is a tiny infectious agent that can only replicate inside living cells, causing various illnesses.
A vaccine is a medical preparation that stimulates immunity against specific diseases, preventing infection.
Immunity is the body's ability to resist infection and disease through its defense systems.
A symptom is a physical or mental sign indicating illness or disease, like fever or pain.
Pain is an unpleasant physical sensation signaling that something is wrong or injured in the body.
Healing is the process by which the body repairs itself after injury or illness, restoring health.
Safety is being protected from danger, harm, or risk through careful actions and precautions.
Danger is the possibility of suffering harm or injury, requiring caution and awareness.
An emergency is a serious, unexpected situation requiring immediate action to prevent harm.
Help is assistance given to someone who needs it, making tasks easier or solving problems.
